node.title: Uber Interview Data Structures and Algorithm

topic.title: Uber Interview Data Structures and Algorithm

post.content:
Question 1: You are given an array with numbers, each round, half of the elements are deleted, the lower number stays, the higher number goes. Comparison is only among consecutive elements. 

Example1: 
1,2,3,4,5,6,7,8
Round1: 1,2,3,4,5,6,7,8
Round2: 1,3,5,7
Round3: 1,5
Round4: 1
1 is winnner

Example2:
1,2,3,4,5,6,7
1,3,5,7
1,5
1
1 is winner
Note: if number is odd, last element just moves to the next round
Print these rounds. 

Question 2: Now you have this algorithm. Generate the configuration of the original array s.t. lower elements are eliminated later. 
Example1: 
Given, n = 7
generated answer: 
1,7,3,5,2,6,4

Example 2: 
n = 8
1,7,3,5,2,6,4,8

Example 3:
n = 9
1,9,5,8,3,7,4,6,2

Second - 
Start from 1 which is winner of final round
keep currElement
Stage 1 - 1
Stage 2 - 1 2(currElement+1), currElement = 2
Stage 3 - 1 3(2+1) 2 4(3+1), currElement = 4
Stage 4 - 1 5(4+1) 3 6 2 7 4 8
. .
. .
. . 
So on. For odd case, push 1 at end, let it go through each round where possible.
Complexity - log(n)*n

I don't think these questions are not completely understandable. In Question 1, the output will be always the smallest element as we are eliminating half of the larger elements. 
In Question 2, do multiple answers exist? If I understand the question correct, Example 1 can also have 7362514 as generated answer.

I am sure some detail is missing here... Care to expand the details of questions?
what verdict you got ?\nsoft hire or hire?
Thanks for sharing. What\'s the location? India?
